sanskrit:
पुरोधसां च मुख्यं मां विद्धि पार्थ बृहस्पतिम्।
सेनानीनामहं स्कन्दः सरसामस्मि सागरः॥
transliteration:
purodhasāṃ ca mukhyaṃ māṃ viddhi pārtha bṛhaspatim;
senānīnām ahaṃ skandaḥ sarasāmasmi sāgaraḥ.
literal_meaning: Of all priests, O Arjuna, know Me as the chief, Brihaspati; of generals I am Skanda; of bodies of water I am the ocean.
contextual_meaning: Krishna names the apex archetypes within three domains—spiritual guidance, strategic leadership, and emotional depth—inviting Arjuna to locate the Supreme inside the highest human exemplars.
psychological_state: Cognitive accommodation of greatness—expanding self-concept to include the trans-personal without losing individual purpose.
trigger_scenario: A mid-career professional meets a legendary mentor, a decorated commander, and the ocean on the same day—suddenly re-evaluating what ‘peak’ means in their own vocation.
anchor_text: See the Supreme in every field’s finest and let that vision guide you.
